Title: Transcription factor module NLP–NIGT1 fine-tunes NITRATE TRANSPORTER2.1 expression

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major nitrogen source for plant growth and nutrition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter-LUC reporter assay in planta; promoter mutagenesis of NIGT1-binding sites; RT-qPCR; analysis in nlp6 nlp7, nigtQ, and phr1 phl1 backgrounds; ODE modeling
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: nitrate inducible; expressed in roots; affected by phosphate status
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: nitrate inducible; peak promoter activity around 80–100 min after nitrate treatment; amplitude reduced by phosphate deficiency in WT
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrate supply after nitrate starvation; phosphate deficiency
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native NRT2.1 promoter (WT and NIGT1-binding-site-mutated versions) driving LUC reporter
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NIGT1.1; NIGT1.2; NIGT1.3; NIGT1.4; NLP6; NLP7
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: dominant high-affinity nitrate uptake gene whose expression output is stabilized by NLP–NIGT1 regulatory module
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NRT2.1 promoter activity is rapidly induced by nitrate and peaks within about 80–100 min. 2. Two NIGT1-binding sites in its promoter are essential for proper temporal repression and output stabilization. 3. Its expression amplitude is buffered across nitrate concentrations by the NLP–NIGT1 feedforward loop. 4. Phosphate deficiency reduces its induction through PHR1/PHL1-dependent signaling.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: without NIGT1-mediated repression, promoter activity becomes more variable and prolonged; direct growth tradeoffs not reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: editing cis-elements in the NRT2.1 promoter offers a route to engineer nitrate uptake dynamics and nutrient response behavior
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: central readout and target gene of the study rather than a regulator; promoter cis-elements for both NLP and NIGT1 are highly conserved among Arabidopsis ecotypes; rice homolog OsNRT2.1 also shows strong conservation of putative NLP/NIGT1 motifs

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major nitrogen source for plant growth and nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root uptake system; whole plant nitrogen acquisition
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: nitrate uptake via transcriptional control of high-affinity nitrate transporter expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression; autorepression within NIGT1 family; incoherent feedforward regulation downstream of NLP
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses NRT2.1 expression; represses NIGT1 expression (auto-repressive pathway)
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: incoherent type I feedforward loop with NLP activating both NRT2.1 and NIGT1; NIGT1 then represses NRT2.1; also includes NIGT1 autorepression
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: nitrate via NLP activation; phosphorus deficiency via PHR1/PHL1-dependent activation of NIGT1 genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: promoter-LUC time-course assays with WT versus mutated NIGT1-binding sites in NRT2.1 promoter; analysis in nigt1 quadruple mutant; RT-qPCR; protoplast transient expression assays; ODE modeling and sensitivity analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: NIGT1.4 is specifically/root-preferentially expressed and strongly influences NRT2.1 expression in roots; NIGT1.1–NIGT1.4 show similar nitrate-induced temporal expression patterns
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: nitrate inducible; induced under phosphorus deficiency through PHR1/PHL1 pathway
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrate supply (0.2–10 mM); nitrate starvation before induction; phosphate deficiency (0–5 µM Pi versus sufficient Pi)
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major buffering repressors that stabilize amplitude and kinetics of NRT2.1 expression across varying nitrate concentrations
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: stabilizes nitrate-induced NRT2.1 expression amplitude across a broad nitrate range and dampens response to changes in NLP activity; loss causes higher and more persistent NRT2.1 promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NIGT1 proteins directly repress NRT2.1 through conserved promoter binding sites. 2. Mutation of NIGT1-binding sites or loss of all four NIGT1 genes increases amplitude and prolongs decline of nitrate-induced NRT2.1 promoter activity. 3. Mathematical modeling and experiments show NIGT1 buffers changes in NLP input and stabilizes nitrate uptake gene output. 4. P deficiency influences NRT2.1 partly through PHR1/PHL1-dependent induction of NIGT1 genes.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: stronger repression could reduce nitrate uptake capacity under some conditions; detailed growth/yield penalty not reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: editing NIGT1 protein properties or NIGT1-binding cis-elements in the NRT2.1 promoter could tune nitrate uptake dynamics and engineer desired nutrient response patterns
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NIGT1.4 was used as representative family member in ODE modeling because of strong effect in roots; functional NIGT1-binding sites in Arabidopsis NRT2.1 promoter are completely conserved among 1,135 ecotypes; putative sites are also highly conserved in rice OsNRT2.1 promoter

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major nitrogen source for plant growth and nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root uptake system; whole plant nitrogen acquisition
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: nitrate-responsive activation of nitrate uptake gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NRT2.1 and induces NIGT1 genes in response to nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: forms incoherent type I feedforward loop by activating both NRT2.1 and its repressors NIGT1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic analysis using nlp6 nlp7-1 mutant carrying NRT2.1pro::LUC; promoter assays; RT-qPCR-supported monitoring; ODE modeling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: NLP6 and NLP7 are major NLP regulators of NRT2.1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: active upon nitrate treatment; high nitrate can partially compensate for loss of NLP6/7 by other NLPs
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: nitrate supply from 0.2 to 10 mM under prior nitrate starvation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major nitrate-responsive activators required for full activation of NRT2.1 especially under low nitrate concentrations
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes nitrate-induced NRT2.1 expression; loss of NLP6/7 reduces peak promoter activity in a nitrate concentration-dependent manner, especially under low nitrate
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLP6 and NLP7 are the main activators controlling the nitrate-responsive NRT2.1 promoter. 2. nlp6 nlp7 mutants show reduced NRT2.1 promoter activation, particularly at low nitrate. 3. NLP input is buffered by downstream NIGT1 repression to stabilize NRT2.1 output.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: loss impairs full activation of nitrate uptake under low nitrate; no broader tradeoffs reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: modulating NLP activity together with NIGT1-mediated repression could optimize nitrate uptake responsiveness across variable soil nitrate levels
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: other NLP family members may compensate at high nitrate concentrations; model predicts active NLP decay strongly influences NRT2.1 response patterns

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: major nitrogen source for plant growth and nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: root uptake system; whole plant nitrogen acquisition
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: cross-regulation of nitrate uptake under phosphate deficiency through induction of NIGT1 genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates NIGT1 gene expression under phosphate deficiency, indirectly reducing NRT2.1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: cross-nutrient regulatory input into the NLP–NIGT1–NRT2.1 network
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: NRT2.1pro::LUC analysis in phr1 phl1 double mutant under different phosphate conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: functionally relevant under phosphate-deficient but not phosphate-sufficient conditions
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: phosphate deficiency combined with nitrate treatment
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: links phosphate status to nitrate uptake gene regulation by enabling P-deficiency-dependent repression of NRT2.1 via NIGT1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: under phosphate deficiency, PHR1/PHL1 reduce nitrate-induced NRT2.1 promoter amplitude indirectly through NIGT1 induction; this effect is lost in phr1 phl1 mutants
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. P deficiency lowers nitrate-induced NRT2.1 promoter activity in WT. 2. This reduction is largely absent in phr1 phl1 mutants. 3. PHR1/PHL1 therefore connect phosphate deficiency signaling to NRT2.1 regulation through NIGT1 induction.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful for engineering integrated N-P nutrient response circuits, though direct nutrient-balance consequences require further study
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PHR1/PHL1 were inactive under phosphate-sufficient conditions in this context; discussed mainly as upstream regulators of NIGT1
